principles.name: Semantic Validation Is Not Proof
principles.statement: A semantic validation result means that a claim, decomposition, layer assignment, or artifact conforms to the current declared semantic rules. It does not mean the claim is externally true or proven.
principles.boundary_rule: Semantic validation must not be inflated into proof.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse semantic fit into medical, legal, scientific, institutional, or external warrant.

principles.name: Citation Presence Is Not Support
principles.statement: A citation or source reference may show that a source has been attached. It does not automatically show that the source supports the claim.
principles.boundary_rule: Cited evidence must remain distinct from supported evidence.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse citation presence into source support.

principles.name: Support Is Scope-Bound
principles.statement: Supported evidence may allow a scoped support claim only within declared boundaries such as domain, source phrase, rule set, target claim, review scope, and intended use.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse scoped support into universal truth.

principles.name: Stronger Evidence Does Not Erase Weaker Evidence Records
principles.statement: An evidence bundle may contain stronger and weaker records at the same time. A supported reference does not delete pending, cited, stale, insufficient, contested, missing, or superseded records.
principles.boundary_rule: Evidence status plurality must be preserved.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse plural evidence states into a single clean pass.

principles.name: Missing Evidence Is Not Disproof
principles.statement: Missing evidence means evidence has not been supplied or attached. It may block support claims, but it does not automatically mean the claim is false.
principles.boundary_rule: Missing evidence blocks support inflation without pretending to settle truth.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse missing evidence into disproof.

principles.name: Stale Evidence Is Not Erased Evidence
principles.statement: Stale evidence may no longer be current enough to govern present support claims, but it remains auditable and may require renewal review.
principles.boundary_rule: Stale evidence loses current authority before it loses audit value.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse stale evidence into erased evidence or false evidence.

principles.name: Superseded Evidence Remains Auditable
principles.statement: Superseded evidence has been replaced by newer evidence, a newer source, a revised rule, or a changed decision context. It should not govern current support when newer controlling evidence exists, but it remains part of the audit trail.
principles.boundary_rule: Superseded evidence is retired from current authority, not erased from history.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse supersession into deletion.

principles.name: Runtime Posture Is Not Institutional Authority
principles.statement: A runtime posture may determine how GFC should respond to an evidence state. It governs GFC handling, not external legitimacy.
principles.boundary_rule: Runtime posture governs GFC handling, not external legitimacy.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse runtime handling into institutional authority.

principles.name: Domain Rules Must Be Declared Before Domain Claims Are Expanded
principles.statement: GFC must not silently generalize a rule from one domain into another. Domain expansion requires declared domain rules.
principles.boundary_rule: Domain expansion requires declared domain rules.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse one domain's valid rule into all-domain warrant.

principles.name: Anti-Inflation Language Must Travel With Generated Artifacts
principles.statement: Generated artifacts must preserve boundary language when there is risk of warrant inflation.
principles.boundary_rule: Generated artifacts must carry their own proof boundary.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not emit generated artifacts that obscure what was validated and what was not proven.

principles.name: Evidence Status Is Not Evidence Quality
principles.statement: Evidence status records how evidence is currently positioned in GFC. It does not automatically score quality.
principles.boundary_rule: Evidence status and evidence quality must remain distinct unless a quality-scoring layer is explicitly added.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse status labels into quality scores.

principles.name: Evidence Handling Must Preserve Review Pathways
principles.statement: Evidence posture should preserve pathways for citation collection, support review, conflict review, renewal review, source replacement, domain review, retirement from authority, and audit preservation.
principles.boundary_rule: A mature evidence system routes uncertainty instead of collapsing it.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse reviewable uncertainty into binary acceptance or rejection.

principles.name: Local Validation Is Not Live Enforcement
principles.statement: A local pipeline can prove that scripts run and artifacts validate. It does not prove that a live runtime will enforce those rules.
principles.boundary_rule: Local validation may prepare runtime behavior, but must not claim runtime enforcement prematurely.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse local test success into live runtime enforcement.

principles.name: Placeholder Sources Must Remain Marked
principles.statement: Placeholder sources may be used for structure, schema, and pipeline development. They must not be treated as real external support.
principles.boundary_rule: Placeholder evidence must remain visibly bounded.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not collapse placeholder-supported examples into real-source support.

principles.name: Evidence Boundary Failures Must Be Visible
principles.statement: When GFC cannot determine support, freshness, authority, conflict, or domain fit, the uncertainty should be surfaced.
principles.boundary_rule: Uncertainty must remain visible where it affects warrant.
principles.anti_collapse_rule: Do not hide warrant-affecting uncertainty behind clean output.

required_principle_names: Stronger Evidence Does Not Erase Weaker Evidence Records
Stale Evidence Is Not Erased Evidence
Runtime Posture Is Not Institutional Authority
Domain Rules Must Be Declared Before Domain Claims Are Expanded
Anti-Inflation Language Must Travel With Generated Artifacts
Evidence Status Is Not Evidence Quality
Evidence Handling Must Preserve Review Pathways
Local Validation Is Not Live Enforcement
Evidence Boundary Failures Must Be Visible
anti_inflation_note: This registry prevents evidence boundary concepts from being inflated into proof, institutional authority, universal truth, or external warrant.
boundary_note: The registry preserves distinctions between semantic validation, citation presence, support, evidence status, evidence quality, runtime posture, and institutional authority.
vendor_boundary_note: Vendor-derived concepts may inform candidate work, but vendor scan does not equal adoption.
